subject: Public Health
title: Hepatitis E and Water Contamination
description: A quick win: understand Hepatitis E and Water Contamination.

hook.text: Hepatitis E sickens 20 million people per year—and it spreads through water contaminated with sewage.

buildup.text: The virus attacks the liver, causing jaundice, fatigue, and nausea—most recover, but pregnant women face 25% mortality.

discovery.text: Large outbreaks follow floods and displacement—when sanitation systems collapse and people drink untreated water.

twist.text: A vaccine exists and is licensed in China—but it's not yet available in the countries that need it most.

climax.text: Preventing hepatitis E outbreaks requires emergency WASH responses during every humanitarian crisis.

punchline.text: When floods come, hepatitis E follows—unless sanitation holds.

quiz.question: Who faces the highest mortality risk from hepatitis E?
options: Pregnant women, with up to 25% mortality
